title: Fireflies Between Us
subtitle: Some memories never fade. Some people never leave.
description: Millhaven, Georgia. Summer 2002. Kit Jenkins is twelve years old, reckless, and convinced that any plan worth having can be figured out along the way. Daisy May Jones has known him long enough to know he's right about that — and wrong about almost everything else. What starts as one long summer of bikes and thunderstorms and condemned water towers becomes the summer that everything quietly begins to change.
shortDescription: A nostalgic coming-of-age story about a small Southern town, a reckless boy, a girl who sees right through him, and the summer neither of them will forget.

mainCharacters.name: Kit Jenkins
mainCharacters.description: Fearless, reckless, charismatic. A natural leader who hides real vulnerability behind confidence and motion.

mainCharacters.name: Daisy May Jones
mainCharacters.description: Smart, grounded, quietly stubborn. She teases Kit constantly and understands him better than anyone.

author: StoryWeaver

chapters.title: The Last Light of Summer
chapters.mood: Some summers stay with you long after the sun goes down.

chapters.title: Something to Lose
chapters.mood: The Henderson house, a close call, and the thing neither of them said after.

chapters.title: The Long Way Home
chapters.mood: Last Saturday before school. Hester's, the park, the walk home that takes longer than it needs to.

chapters.title: Away Message
chapters.mood: Seventh grade changes the rules. Some things you can't unsay. Some things you don't have to.

chapters.title: Offline
chapters.mood: He knew what that place was. He went anyway. She found out from someone else.

chapters.title: Somebody
chapters.mood: Freshman year. A new name in her conversations. A feeling Kit doesn't have a word for yet — but he's done pretending it isn't there.

chapters.title: Almost
chapters.mood: Sophomore year. The table changes. The dance. Three seconds on the floor where the whole thing almost became something else.

chapters.title: Before August
chapters.mood: Senior year. USC and Minnesota. The Molly conversation, finally. Prom. A kiss that doesn't happen — and two people who finally say the thing out loud.

chapters.title: The Distance
chapters.mood: Two different cities. Texts across time zones. A fight that needed to happen. A phone call that lasted until she fell asleep on the line. December. Coming home. Finally giving it a name.

chapters.title: This Kind of Night
chapters.mood: July Fourth. The field. Ten years in the making. Fireflies, fireworks, and the moment they stopped waiting.

chapters.title: On Purpose
chapters.mood: A film made in secret. A question asked from a thousand miles away. Eight sessions and everything they needed to say. Four months.

chapters.title: The Field
chapters.mood: A wedding. A city. A loss that broke them open and a love that held. The film that was really about his father. A mother who stayed. And a field in April, with the first fireflies of the season, where everything that had ever mattered came back home.
